channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: From the desert to the Strip: How the election's fight over immigration may upend Nevada's economy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the remote Nevada desert, the Baker Ranch couldn't survive without immigrant guest workers who come every year from Mexico.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blinken heads to the Middle East for the 11th time since the Gaza war, but truce prospects uncertain
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken is heading again to the Middle East, making his 11th trip to the region since the war in Gaza erupted last year and as Israel steps up attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas inmate Robert Roberson NOT testifying before lawmakers Monday in surprise announcement during committee hearing
item.description: In a surprise announcement by lawmakers claimed during a Committee on Criminal Jurisprudence Monday, that he would not be testifying before lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Robert Arnold, Bryce Newberry, Karen Araiza, Ahmed Humble, KPRC 2

item.title: Central Park Five sues Donald Trump for jogger case remarks made at presidential debate
item.description: The men formerly known as the Central Park Five before they were exonerated are suing Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Terry Tang, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris raises more than $220 million in September, outraising Trump 3-to-1
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris’s campaign raised nearly $222 million in September, raising more than three times as much as former President Donald Trump’s campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court won’t revive Michael Cohen’s lawsuit against Trump claiming retaliatory imprisonment
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court rejected an appeal Monday from Michael Cohen, who wanted to hold his former boss ex-president Donald Trump liable for a jailing he said was retaliation for writing a tell-all memoir.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court rejects lawyer Michael Cohen lawsuit against Trump over alleged retaliation
item.description: The Supreme Court rejected without comment an appeal from Michael Cohen to revive a lawsuit against former President Trump on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court turns away challenge to consumer safety agency’s structure
item.description: The Supreme Court announced Monday it won’t take up a case that could have delivered another blow to federal agencies. Two conservative groups sought to challenge the structure of the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC), which oversees making sure the country’s consumer products are safe.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New report shows clear frontrunner in Harris, Trump campaign cash race
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris dramatically outraised and outspent former President Trump in September and entered the final full month of the presidential campaign with a large cash advantage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arnold Palmer's daughter reacts to Donald Trump's references to her father
item.description: One of the late golf legend Arnold Palmer's daughters calls Donald Trump's references to her father's genitalia “a poor choice of approaches" to honoring his memory, adding that she wasn't upset by the remarks. “There’s nothing much to say.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The race is so close. Here's what happens if there's a 269-269 Electoral College tie
item.description: It’s very unlikely — but theoretically possible — that the presidential election ends with an Electoral College tie. If it did come to pass, here's how the next president would be determined.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez, MPR News

item.title: Pair of suburban-centered Minnesota congressional districts attract differing levels of attention
item.description: Minnesota’s east-suburban 2nd Congressional District and west-suburban 3rd Congressional District have traits in common, but they’re not being contested in similar fashion this year.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Inflation would be higher under Trump: Economists
item.description: The percentage of economists who believe it rose from the Journal’s last survey, which was before both candidates released their major policy proposals.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Donald Trump stops by primetime NFL matchup between the Jets and Steelers to cap dizzying weekend
item.description: Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump attended Sunday night's NFL game between the Pittsburgh Steelers and New York Jets as the guest of an unidentified suite holder at Acrisure Stadium.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Montana GOP candidate who could flip control of Senate nagged by claims he lied about bullet wound
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — A former Park Service ranger said Friday that U.S. Senate hopeful Tim Sheehy of Montana has been lying about a bullet wound that the candidate said came from fighting in Afghanistan — going public with an accusation that has nagged the Republican’s campaign for months.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris says Trump is 'cruel' at Georgia rally as she spotlights abortion restrictions
item.description: Kamala Harris has said former President Trump is “cruel” for how he talked about the family of a Georgia mother who died after waiting for treatment for complications from an abortion pill.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Early voting kicks off in Las Vegas
item.description: As early voting begins in Southern Nevada, Las Vegans are considering who will get their vote ahead of Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Tisminezky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Child labor violations on the rise, problem could get worse: report
item.description: Between 2011 and 2020, the rate of injuries among workers under 18 nearly doubled, a recent report says.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Harris and Trump hold competing rallies in Michigan
item.description: While the two candidates have been crisscrossing the swing states for weeks, this is the first time they are literally crossing paths, with each of them holding events in the suburbs north of Detroit.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea, Asma Khalid, and Eric McDaniel, MPR News

item.title: Trump expresses frustration about calls to deploy Haley
item.description: “But let me just tell you: Nikki Haley and I fought, and I beat her by 50, 60, 90 points. I beat her in her own state. I beat Nikki badly.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How Republicans mainstreamed the baseless idea of noncitizen voting in 2024
item.description: Donald Trump and his allies have zeroed in on the baseless claim that Democrats are encouraging newly arrived migrants to vote for them in the 2024 election. There is no evidence of a plot like this.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block and Miles Parks, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk has given $75 million, so far, to put Donald Trump back in the White House
item.description: Elon Musk is among the largest financial supporters of Donald Trump's reelection efforts, new FEC filings show. He and two other megadonors collectively gave $300 million to support Trump.
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel, MPR News
